name: acs_pums_wy2022
description: Random 400-row subsample of the 2022 American Community Survey (ACS) 1-Year Public Use Microdata Sample (PUMS) person-level file for Wyoming, filtered to employed adults (age >= 18, positive hours worked and income).
source: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2022 1-Year PUMS
license: Public domain (U.S. federal government work, 17 U.S.C. § 105) - no redistribution ambiguity, unlike star98.
sampling: Randomly subsampled (numpy default_rng seed 42) from 3139 rows matching the filter, out of 5962 total Wyoming person records in the original 2022 ACS 1-Year PUMS file.

target.name: log_pincp
target.description: Natural log of total person's income (PINCP) in the past 12 months, in nominal dollars (ADJINC inflation adjustment not applied - this is a single-year extract used only to demonstrate sample_weight, not for real income analysis).

weight.description: Census person weight: the number of people in the population this sample record represents (ranges roughly 3 to 600+ in this subsample). This is a genuine survey sampling weight, not a derived precision weight like MATHTOT in star98 - the canonical real-world use case sample_weight was requested for (see pyoperon issue #17).

feature_codes.SCHL: Educational attainment (ACS code; higher value = more schooling)
feature_codes.WKHP: Usual hours worked per week in the past 12 months

validated.method: Compared plain OLS vs WLS (numpy) on log(PINCP) ~ AGEP+SCHL+WKHP+SEX+MAR weighted by PWGTP.
validated.result: Each fit wins on its own objective (unweighted MSE 0.6352 vs 0.6433 for OLS vs WLS; weighted MSE 0.7252 vs 0.7173), confirming the weighting has a genuine, expected real-world effect.
